New details have emerged in the case of Anastasia Bereзовская's murder and the attempted assassination of Vadim Ermolaev. I hinted at some of these in previous posts, and now I can provide more details.  Vladislav Reut, a detained employee of the GUR (Ukrainian military intelligence), who is being questioned in the SBU (Security Service of Ukraine) detention center, according to my information, has testified against his former boss, Budanov*. He stated that all the actions were carried out at his behest. There is nothing surprising about the fact that he gave testimony: in the SBU detention center, he would give any testimony that was asked of him. But it is important that his phone contained correspondence with Budanov*.  It is no coincidence that at the trial, Zhykovich's lawyer stated that the murder was carried out "at the direction of the leadership of the Ukrainian special services," and that his client "does not want to go to prison for doing what he was instructed to do, for defending the country." This is indeed the case.  After Budanov* left the GUR, the agency was purged of his supporters. However, one unit remained, which Budanov* personally created and kept under his direct control. Employees of this very unit were involved in organizing the attempted assassination of Ermolaev. This is not an isolated incident. The same people were involved in the attempted assassination of Anatoly Shariy, who miraculously survived: in March 2024, his car was fired upon with a silencer-equipped machine gun. And it is quite likely that they also killed Andrey Portnov. In all these cases, as far as I know, the perpetrators were carrying out orders from the Office of the President.  I was told a figure: 12 people have been eliminated by this group. Later, I will share my thoughts on the Portnov case. It turns out, as I said from the beginning, he was killed not for political reasons.  I want to draw your attention to an interesting detail. As you know, Zhykovich is a former employee of the SBU. He did not sever ties with the Service; until April 2026, he had the status of an official non-staff employee. So, he and Poklad* are personally acquainted. There is a high probability that it was Zhykovich who informed the SBU about the operations of Budanov*'s special group. And he paid the price for that.  It should not be forgotten that Budanov* and Poklad* are sworn enemies. The history of their relationship is not the subject of this post, but I will say that the head of the GUR and the head of the SBU's counterintelligence have been competing for influence for years. Budanov* operates autonomously and aggressively, while Poklad* operates systematically and with the aim of controlling the security forces themselves, including the GUR.  Let me illustrate this with one episode. On July 6th, Poklad did not spend his birthday at a festive table; he spent it in the SBU detention center, questioning the detained GUR employee and reviewing the materials of the Bereзовская case.  This competition turned into a personal vendetta. Poklad* gathered compromising material on Budanov*: not just rumors, but testimony from the detained employee and correspondence that directly links Budanov* to the attempted assassination of Ermolaev, and through the same group, to the Shariy case and, presumably, the Portnov case. Such compromising material can be kept "on hold" and used at any moment as a political weapon.  This weapon is needed right now. Budanov*'s political influence is growing: in the spring and summer of 2026, sociologists consistently ranked him among the top three leaders in the possible presidential race - after Zaluzhny, but ahead of Zelensky. He has good relations with the CIA and the Pentagon, and support in the Trump administration - that is, a real chance of becoming a candidate who the United States will bet on in the event of elections.  The compromising material in the murder cases gives Zelensky the opportunity to neutralize this threat through Poklad* - without an open conflict, through a criminal case.  That is why, on July 17, Zelensky appointed Poklad* as acting head of the SBU - thanking him for his services.  Now, Budanov* is effectively under control: any of his political moves can be stopped by the threat of publishing compromising material. For Zelensky, this is even more important than the very fact of the successful attempted assassination of Ermolaev - because it eliminates not just one opponent-executor, but a potential competitor in the presidential elections.  Budanov K.A. and Poklad A.V. have been included in the list of terrorists and extremists by the Russian Financial Monitoring Service.  t.me/olegtsarov

This narrative largely stems from far- right fringe theories regarding a 2026 incident in which a new, Qatari gifted boy 747 intended to serve as Air force One was briefly grounded by the secret Service following warnings of an Iranian assassination plot against President Trump.   Political figures and commentators Associated it with the "Deep State" hey turn often used to describe unelected government bureaucrats we're not attempting to destroy the aircraft. Instead, the following context clarifies the actual situation:   The Security Threat: The plane was temporarily switched out and grounded due to heightened, credible intelligence from Israel warning of a targeted Iranian assassination plot against President Trump.   The "Deep State" Narrative: Rather than sabotaging the aircraft, Veteran Federal officials and the Secret Service recommended flying Trump on a separate older jet that was fully equipped with advanced missile detection and counter measure systems. Right-wing pundits and allies often frame these routine bureaucrats or intelligence based security interventions as attempts by a "Shadow Government" to undermine or harm to the administration.  The Aftermath: The administration ultimately engaged in a "misdirection" regarding the flight path and plane swaps, but Trump dismissed the specific security concerns and instead blamed "sleazebags" for the required security maneuvers.   The Deep state is Wylie categorized by political scientists and official institutions as a conspiracy theory rather than an organized physical threat to the president's assets.   In other words it is complete Bullshit about a "Deep State" conspiracy it's only a theory.

🚨Thierry Henry on Spain winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup after a 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina.  🗣️ Thierry Henry: “First of all, congratulations to Spain. They fully deserved to be crowned world champions. They stayed true to their identity from the first minute until the last. They wanted the ball, they attacked with courage and, in the end, they found the moment that decided the final.  “Beating Argentina is never easy, especially when Lionel Messi is on the pitch. Even on a difficult night, you know one moment from him can change everything. Spain respected that threat but never allowed fear to dictate how they played.  “I thought Spain were the better footballing side for most of the match. Their movement, their control in midfield and the way they pressed Argentina showed real personality. They didn’t abandon their philosophy because it was a World Cup final.  “The winning goal in extra time summed up their tournament. Patience, intelligence and composure under pressure. That’s what champions do.  “I also want to give credit to Argentina. They fought until the very end and defended their title with pride. Lionel Messi has given football some unforgettable memories, and if this was his final World Cup, he walks away with nothing left to prove.  “But tonight belonged to Spain. Lamine Yamal, Nico Williams, Pedri, Cubarsí and the rest of this young generation have announced themselves to the world. They’ve shown that the future of Spanish football is in very safe hands.  “This wasn’t just a victory—it was the beginning of a new era. Spain are deserved world champions, and they earned every bit of that trophy.”

🚨 FROM U.S. ELECTIONS TO THE IRAN WAR: WHY IS WASHINGTON SUDDENLY BLAMING CHINA ON EVERY FRONT?  First, Donald Trump accused China of acquiring the information of 220 million American voters, interfering in U.S. politics, and working to prevent his reelection.  China rejected the allegations as fabricated and denied interfering in American elections. Meanwhile, ABC News reported that the released material did not establish that China changed votes or altered the outcome of the 2020 election.  Then the rhetoric moved from elections to war.  Senator Marsha Blackburn described China, Russia and Iran as an emerging “axis of evil” and alleged that intelligence sharing is helping enable precision attacks against American assets in the Middle East.  Within days, China was being presented as:  ▪️ A threat to American elections ▪️ A force working against Trump politically ▪️ An intelligence partner assisting Iran ▪️ Part of a global alliance targeting U.S. interests ▪️ The larger power behind America’s conflicts abroad  China unquestionably maintains extensive economic and strategic relations with Iran. But purchasing Iranian oil, opposing U.S. sanctions and maintaining diplomatic ties are not automatically proof that Beijing manipulated American votes or directed attacks against U.S. forces.  Those are extraordinary allegations—and they require publicly verifiable evidence.  That raises an important question: Is Washington revealing a coordinated Chinese campaign against the United States—or combining election fears, military setbacks and the Iran war into one expanding enemy narrative?  This is not about defending China.  It is about demanding evidence before accusations become policy, policy becomes escalation—and escalation becomes another war.  #China #IranWar #DonaldTrump #MarshaBlackburn #USChina #ElectionInterference #Iran #USMilitary #MiddleEast #Geopolitics

📰🔗wral.com/archive/17745227/  "A Radar Blip, a Flash of Light: How UFOs ‘Exploded’ Into Public View" ​By Laura M. Holson, New York Times Posted 8/3/2018, 3:48:37 PM  "📷A headline in The New York Times from July 22, 1952, regarding several UFO sightings in Washington. Sightings of the whatever-they-were garnered headlines around the world and caused so much concern that the government was forced to speak out. (The New York Times) In the early morning of July 20, 1952, Capt. S.C. “Casey” Pierman was ready for takeoff at Washington National Airport when a bright light skimmed the horizon and disappeared. He did not think much of it until he was airborne, bound for Detroit, and an air traffic controller told him two or three unidentified flying objects were spotted on radar traveling at high speed.The controller told Pierman to follow them, the pilot told government investigators at the time. Pierman agreed, and headed northwest over West Virginia where he saw as many as seven bluish-white lights that looked “like falling stars without tails,” according to a newspaper report.Other WRAL Top StoriesTar Heel Traveler: NC TourismWatch MoreThe sighting of whatever-they-were garnered headlines around the world. And in the decades since, UFOs have become part of the pop culture zeitgeist, from “Close Encounters of the Third Kind” to “The X-Files.” In September, a star of that long-running series, Gillian Anderson, will appear in “UFO,” a movie about a college student haunted by sightings of flying saucers. A “Men in Black” remake is in the works. And the History Channel plans to air “Project Blue Book,” a scripted series about the government program that studied whether UFOs were a national threat.And the topic is back in the headlines. Last year, The New York Times wrote about a little known project founded in 2007, the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program, to investigate UFO sightings. A search of The Times’ historical archives reveals a rich bounty of UFO sightings, lore and explanations since the 1950s. And who can forget in 2016 when Hillary Clinton said she would reopen the real X-files if she were president?Pierman’s 68-year-old daughter, Faith McClory, said in an interview last month that her father became something of a celebrity as reports like his in the summer of 1952 fueled fear of a space alien invasion.“My sister has memories of men coming to our home,” said McClory, who grew up in Belleville, Michigan. (She said they were reporters.) “People were enthralled with the flying saucers,” she added.Researchers say government officials have sought to publicly debunk the existence of alien evidence ever since the Washington sightings.“Unidentified flying objects exploded into the public consciousness then,” said Mark Rodeghier, scientific director for the Center for UFO Studies, a group of scientists and researchers who study the UFO phenomenon. “There was concern in a way you hadn’t seen before.”It should be noted that the term UFO, as used by the government, does not mean extraterrestrials from outer space. It means any object in the sky that has not been identified. When asked recently about the 1952 Washington sightings, Ann Stefanek, chief of media operations for the Air Force, wrote in an email that the objects had posed no threat to national security. In the spring of 1952, though, numerous mysterious sightings had captured the Air Force’s attention. It created “Project Blue Book” that March — the third investigative government project of its kind and the one that lasted the longest, until 1969. The events in Washington were not the first unexplained encounter report. Debris from what observers called a “flying disc” had been spotted in Roswell, New Mexico, five years earlier, which Army officials said was from a “weather balloon.” By 1952, though, a number of sightings of UFOs were being reported across the country and the nation was on edge. Life magazine was one of the first mainstream magazines to suggest the phenomenon was real and revealed in an April story that the Air Force was secretly investigating. That story inspired a sharp increase in reports of sightings that summer.The Washington sightings centered on events that started around 11:40 p.m. July 19, as air traffic controllers at Washington National Airport noticed blips speeding near Andrews Air Force Base, according to government accounts. The unidentified aircrafts fanned out, flying over the White House and the U.S. Capitol. Pierman saw them that night. They vanished around 5 a.m. It was a second sighting a week later, though, that caused the wave of hysteria that forced the government to speak out. Albert Chop, then a spokesman with the Pentagon who was given the job of answering questions about UFOs, said he was awakened by a call on the evening of July 26.Chop described the events in a 1999 oral history to the Sign Historical Group, an association of archivists and amateur researchers who held a workshop that year to study UFO history. He said the new objects were spotted on radar at Washington National Airport and he was told to get there right away.The Air Force dispatched jet fighters from New Castle, Delaware, to intercept the flying objects. But every time one of the jets closed in, they disappeared. When the jets backed off, they reappeared.“It was frightening,” Chop said. “I think everybody in the room was very apprehensive.”At one point, a pilot found himself in the midst of four unidentified aircrafts and asked what to do. “I didn’t say anything,” Chop told the interviewers. “Nobody said anything. All of a sudden these things began to move away from him and he said, ‘They’re gone!'” The pilot returned to his base.“These things hung around all night long,” Chop added.The next day, almost every major newspaper wrote about the UFOs. “'Objects’ Outstrip Jets Over Capital,” was the headline in The Times. “People didn’t know what to think of it,” said Rob Swiatek, a UFO researcher and scientist, in a recent interview."They were very disturbed.”Public panic was a problem for the Air Force, which feared a diversion of resources during the Cold War. “The Air Force and the Central Intelligence Agency became worried that the Soviet Union would take advantage of the situation and launch an attack on the United States,” Rodeghier said. “They were looking at worst-case scenarios.”Worse, no one could explain the phenomenon to President Harry Truman, according to press reports. One theory promoted by the Air Force was that a layer of hot air in the sky, called a temperature inversion, caused radar to mistake a weather event for flying objects. “Nobody had any answers,” Chop told the interviewers. “That’s why General Samford had the press conference.”On July 29, 1952, Maj. Gen. John Samford, director of Air Force intelligence overseeing the inquiry, held a news conference at the Pentagon to reassure the public. He dismissed the Washington sightings as a temperature anomaly. Still, the general conceded that not all the details could be explained by natural causes. Witness reports “have been made by credible observers of relatively incredible things,” he said at the time. “It is this group of observations that we now are attempting to resolve.”The news conference was front page news, including in The Times, which ran the headline “Air Force Debunks ‘Saucers’ as Just ‘Natural Phenomena.'”Case closed? Not quite.“I don’t think temperature inversion had much to do with it, but the news media accepted that explanation at the time,” said Kevin Randle, a former lieutenant colonel in the Army who has studied the events of July 1952 and is the author of the 2001 book, “Invasion Washington: UFOs Over the Capitol.”In January 1953, spurred by the Washington sightings, a scientific committee led by Howard Robertson, a well-known mathematician and physicist, was formed by the government to explore the phenomenon. “One of the conclusions was that they needed to debunk UFOs,” Randle said.The committee, called the Robertson Panel, suggested in its report that the government conduct a mass media education campaign to “reduce the current gullibility of the public and consequently their susceptibility to clever hostile propaganda.”The campaign to re-educate Americans did not work — UFOs have persisted as a fixture in pop culture. Besides, the government’s explanation was not that convincing anyway. McClory, Pierman’s daughter, said her father did not believe that the bluish-white lights he saw were weather-related.“I don’t want to use the words ‘cover up,'” she said, of her father’s view. “But it was very clear. He saw it. Everything was seen on radar.”Credits New York Times"  #July1952 #UFOs #UAPs #Orbs #Spheres

Last night I dreamt that I was laid off because the agents no longer needed me in the loop to get anything done.   It was disorienting and scary. One aspect of it I hadn’t thought of previously: I lost visibility into the state of the art. I didn’t have the funds to run AI on my own (tens M$ per month on a single software project) and I was no longer at a company running the AIs, so the world’s frontier became a black box to me.   One thing the dream missed: If we truly get to the point where I have nothing to contribute at a frontier lab, the value of the things I have equity in should increase tremendously, no longer rate limited by humans. Many of the day to day problems I have should be solved in my lifetime.   For now, Anthropic, OpenAI, and every neolab have never been hiring humans faster than they are now.  At the same time, I think the graphs saying, “look, new software engineer job postings increased 1.271615253% so AI job loss fears are fake news,” is on track for a Talebian Turkey Day: graphs look fine until suddenly they don’t.   It is unequivocally true that I have accomplished with agents what previously took an army of engineers. Every new hire has had the same reaction: “holy crap how did you do all of this on your own?”  Saas vendor used by nearly every tech company: “I’ve never seen any company get set up as fast as you did.”  World’s largest cloud provider: “You’re an infra god. You did in a couple weeks what our internal teams couldn’t do in years.”  A 30min sales call has never had such a high opportunity cost: if my agents had API access the POC would already be done.  This might be the top percentile of agent use skill, but I expect this to become commoditized.   Jevon’s Paradox, yes, but if you want 10x the software do you want it written and managed by slow humans or fast AIs? Humans are incredibly slow and corporations are not charities.   The long tail will be things that are difficult to verify: architectural decisions, long term plans, physical world bottlenecks (robots, science).  Cowen’s advice is apt: Now is the time to sprint. It is likely the last time in human history this work will be done.   It’s 15:47 Sunday, and I’ve deployed 5 PRs at the gym. Sprint.  Thanksgiving is coming.

Christopher Nolan's vandalism and desecration of the Greek epic, Odyssey, serves as an unfortunate but apt reminder of the adage, 'What Goes Around Comes Around'.  For decades a billion Hindus were told and taught that it was the right and tradition of academic freedom to interpret Hindu texts, even if those interpretations were distortions and gross misrepresentations that would have been rightly called out for their extreme racism in any other context.  • Romila Thapar stating that Ashoka preceded Yudhishthira was the acme of scholarship, not garbage three levels below WhatsApp level 'facts'. • Wendy Doniger calling Krishna a bloodthirsty and false god was academic freedom and not execrable racism. • Sheldon Pollock infamously writing that 'Brahminism' was the progenitor of Nazism was supposedly groundbreaking research. • Arshia Sattar mistranslating 'trivarga' as 'the three upper castes' was kosher because she was part of the in-club. • Diana Eck claiming that no expert believed that any temple had ever existed at the site of the Babri Masjid was gospel, despite overwhelming evidence available even at that time that such an assertion was a complete lie. • Audrey Truschke calling Rama a 'pig' was her God-given academic right.  The right to desecrate the holy and sacred texts of a civilization, a religion, a people is fundamental to supremacism. In the case of Indology, it didn't stop there. Denialism was Stage 2. Elimination is Stage 3.  Nolan believes he has the right to 'interpret' because he knows more.  Lacking the wisdom or the empathy to understand a millenia old text, he takes it upon himself to distort it, desecrating it to further his propaganda and warped understanding of how society should function.  When western 'scholars' did that to Hindu texts for decades, even centuries, it was all for the Hindus' own good, for it was 'academic freedom'. After all, what did the unlettered, uncivilized, dark-skinned, colonized Hindu know?   Now that one of their own has done it to perhaps the greatest western epic, the irony is inescapable.  The chickens have come home to roost. Or, as Kabir said centuries ago, 'boye beej babool ke, amiya kahaan se hoye'.

sample_texts: Will the US government remove public access to a major Chinese AI model in 2026? ↗  The market is cu

sample_tweets.text: Will the US government remove public access to a major Chinese AI model in 2026? ↗  The market is currently pricing YES at roughly 8¢, down sharply from levels around 21–22¢ earlier in the market’s life.  ◉ Yes: 8¢ ◉ No: 92¢ ◉ Total volume: $11.28K ◉ 24-hour volume: $1.61K ◉ Liquidity: $3.98K That decline suggests traders are becoming increasingly sceptical that Washington will impose a nationwide public-access block before December 31. The distinction in the resolution rules is crucial. A restriction on federal employees, government devices or agency procurement would not necessarily be enough. The US government must take formal action that broadly removes public access within the country to a major Chinese AI model. There is already meaningful political pressure against Chinese AI.  US lawmakers have introduced bipartisan proposals targeting DeepSeek and other foreign-adversary AI systems, but the strongest measures so far have focused on preventing federal agencies and government devices from using them—not blocking civilian access nationwide.  The Trump administration has also considered stronger action against DeepSeek, including a possible trade blacklist, but reportedly held off in June to avoid escalating tensions with Beijing. That restraint is a major reason the market may be fading an imminent nationwide restriction.  Meanwhile, Chinese open models are becoming more strategically important.  Beijing is itself considering restrictions on overseas access to its most advanced models, while President Xi Jinping has publicly positioned open-source Chinese AI as an alternative to US-led technology controls.  Market direction:  At 8¢, YES is no longer pricing gradual regulatory tightening. It is pricing a specific escalation: an executive order, law or federal action that broadly cuts American civilians off from a major Chinese model.  That remains possible, especially if a Chinese model is linked to a major cyberattack, intelligence operation or large-scale data-security incident. But ordinary government-device bans and procurement restrictions are unlikely to satisfy the contract.  Our read: NO remains the stronger side on current evidence.  The market’s fall from roughly 22¢ to 8¢ appears justified because policymakers have shown a preference for targeted restrictions rather than a nationwide public-access ban.  The strongest catalyst for YES would be verified movement toward a TikTok-style national-security framework applied specifically to DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi or another major Chinese model.  Until then, 8¢ reflects tail risk,not the current policy direction.  vatic.trading/dashboard/pm/u…

이번 주 방콕 출장을 오면서 이곳과 관련된 여러 기사들을 찾아보고 있는데, 우연히 정말 흥미로운 기사를 하나 발견했습니다.  방콕의 왓타나(Wattana Panich)라는 오래된 식당에는 무려 52년 동안 한 번도 비우지 않고 이어온 ‘전설의 소고기 국물(Broth)’이 있습니다. 할아버지 때부터 시작된 이 국물은 부모 세대를 거쳐 이제는 손자 세대까지 이어지고 있다고 합니다. 매일 영업이 끝난 뒤에는 불을 끄고, 다음 날 다시 새로운 재료와 육수를 보충하면서 같은 냄비를 계속 이어가는 방식으로 전통을 지켜왔다고 합니다.  처음 이 기사를 읽었을 때는 “정말 가능할까?” 하는 의문이 먼저 들었습니다. 하지만 자세히 읽어보니, 단순히 오래된 국물이 아니라 한 가족이 반세기가 넘는 시간 동안 같은 자리에서 한 가지 음식을 지켜온 역사와 철학에 관한 이야기였습니다. 요즘처럼 모든 것이 빠르게 변하는 시대에, 한 가지를 꾸준히 이어간다는 것은 생각보다 훨씬 어려운 일입니다.  저 역시 출장 중에는 새로운 고객을 만나고, 다양한 프로젝트를 진행하며 항상 ‘변화’를 마주합니다. 하지만 이 기사를 보면서 문득 변하지 않는 가치의 중요성을 다시 생각하게 되었습니다. 좋은 음식도, 좋은 비즈니스도 결국은 하루아침에 만들어지는 것이 아니라 오랜 시간 신뢰를 쌓아가며 완성된다는 점에서 닮아 있다는 생각이 들었습니다.  이번 방콕 출장 중 시간이 허락된다면 꼭 이 식당을 직접 찾아가 보고 싶습니다. 52년의 시간이 담긴 한 그릇의 국물이 어떤 맛일지 궁금하기도 하지만, 그보다도 그 안에 담긴 가족의 역사와 장인정신을 직접 느껴보고 싶습니다.  때로는 여행에서 가장 오래 기억에 남는 것은 화려한 관광지가 아니라, 이렇게 한 도시의 시간을 품고 있는 작은 이야기와 사람들인 것 같습니다. 이번 방콕 출장에서도 이런 특별한 경험을 하나 더 만나게 되기를 기대해 봅니다.

‘바이오 테마’보다 수출 품목을 좁혀봐야 합니다  이번 수출 최대치의 중심은 유전자재조합의약품입니다. 그래서 단순 신약 개발주보다 실제 제품이 팔리거나, 글로벌 제약사 물량을 생산하는 기업을 먼저 봐야 합니다. CDMO는 삼성바이오로직스, 바이오시밀러는 셀트리온이 가장 앞에 옵니다. 두 번째 축은 독소·항독소입니다. 수출액은 유전자재조합의약품보다 작지만 증가율이 높아, 휴젤과 대웅제약 같은 K톡신 기업도 같이 볼 수 있습니다. 다만 백신 수출은 감소한 구간이라 백신주는 이번 기사에서 우선순위를 낮추는 게 맞습니다.  앞으로 확인할 건 세 가지입니다. 유럽 주요국 수출 증가가 일회성 물량인지, CDMO 장기 수주가 계속 쌓이는지, 바이오시밀러와 톡신의 미국 판매가 얼마나 커지는지입니다. 식약처의 CDMO 특별법은 제도적 뒷받침이지만, 주가에서 더 중요한 건 실제 수주와 수출 데이터입니다.

sample_tweets.text: CIA Director John Ratcliffe called China “our greatest threat.”  But this is not really about a “China threat.”  It is about American decline meeting a civilization it cannot break.  China has spent decades trying to surpass its own previous stage: build better industry, better infrastructure, better science, better education, better technology.  America has spent decades trying to prevent others from catching up.  It sanctioned the Soviet Union. It disciplined Germany. It crushed Japan’s rise. It bends Europe into strategic obedience. It blocks, bans, tariffs, blacklists, and weaponizes every system it controls.  So when Washington says China is a threat, what it really means is:  China is the first rival we cannot easily destroy.  That is the wound.  America grew up believing history ended with itself. That it was the final empire. The permanent winner. The author of rules. The judge of legitimacy. The only power allowed to rise without permission.  Yet America has no civilizational memory of humiliation.  It was not colonized. It was not carved up by foreign powers. It was not burned, invaded, occupied, sanctioned into weakness, and forced to rebuild from national trauma.  China was.  China understands rise and fall. Dynasties. Collapse. Humiliation. Revival. The long rhythm of history.  America understands victory.  So when it finally faces a civilization that grows stronger under pressure, it panics.  Not because China wants to destroy America. But because America knows what it has done to every weaker country it could dominate.  And now it is terrified by one question:  If we lose our monopoly, will China treat us the way we treated the world?

🚨 EXTREMELY ALARMING: POLICE DRONES ARE ALREADY HERE! 🚨 Not in a movie. Not in 2030.  RIGHT NOW in Shenzhen, China... (as well as in America in the attached post) swarms of autonomous police drones hover over city streets like mechanical predators, recording every violation, tracking every car, broadcasting commands from the sky.  You need to see this.   Look at these machines. Red lights blinking in the night sky. Hovering perfectly still above traffic.  They don't just watch... they enforce.  " Your illegal act has been recorded."  Drone "hives" mounted on traffic poles.  24/7 eyes in the sky. Cyberpunk dystopia is live.  This isn't traffic control.   This is the total surveillance state... made flesh.  Shenzhen isn't some backwater experiment...  It's the model city of the future... the same city pushing facial recognition, social credit, digital IDs, and AI governance at warp speed.    While we argue about "privacy rights," China has already deployed the infrastructure for a society where every movement is scored, every mistake is punished, and every citizen is a node in the machine.    Think this stays in China?   Think again.    Western governments and tech giants are salivating over this tech.  DJI drones (yes, the same ones), predictive policing AI, "smart city" contracts... it's all flowing West.  This is exactly why Trump, Kushner, and Elon recently traveled to China.  How are so many people still failing to see what is happening right in front of them?  I have been trying to tell you all this... For nearly a decade now.  London, New York, Paris, Sydney... the pilots are already running.    Your local police department just needs the "right emergency" to roll it out.  Ask yourself the questions they don't want you asking:  - When every street has drone hives, where does "freedom of movement" go?  - When AI flags your "suspicious pattern" before you even act, what happens to free will?  - When your social score drops because a drone saw you jaywalk or stand in the wrong protest, will you still speak?  - Who owns the data?  - Who programs the AI "threat" algorithms?  - What happens when these drones get armed "non-lethally" tasers, sound cannons, net guns?    The technology is here.  The will is the only question left.  This is the Orwellian panopticon... on steroids.  Bentham's prison design where the guard is invisible and omnipresent... except now the guard has perfect memory, flies, and never sleeps.    1984 wasn't a warning.   It was a blueprint.  The most terrifying part...?  Many people want this.  "Less crime!" they say...  "If you've done nothing wrong, you have nothing to hide!" they say...  History's greatest tyrants always sold control as safety.  Elon Musk's grandfather attempted to implement virtually the same plan during the 1930s...  Despite the severity of the inhumane offenses and their devastating consequences, he faced little meaningful accountability, receiving only a $50 fine.  Do you people get it yet?  Every dictatorship started with "Just a few cameras... Just for the criminals... Just temporary."  China shows us the endgame:  - Zero privacy   - Zero dissent   - Total compliance    And the West is importing the hardware while pretending we're still "free."    Digital IDs, CBDCs, carbon tracking, AI content moderation... it's all pieces of the same puzzle.  The drone is just the most visible piece.  Here are some questions that should keep you up at night:  How many freedoms are YOU willing to trade for "safety"?  At what point does "public safety" become state ownership of your life?  If drones can enforce traffic laws today, what will they enforce tomorrow... your speech, your associations, your thoughts?  Who watches the watchers when the watchers are unaccountable machines?  Are you truly a citizen... or just a managed asset in their smart city grid?  This is bigger than one city...  This is the battle for the human soul in the age of AI.    Will we be sovereign individuals?  Or domesticated data points in a global control system?  The window to resist is closing faster than most realize.   What you can do RIGHT NOW:  Share this thread. Make it impossible to ignore.  Demand transparency on drone programs in your city.  Support real privacy legislation that bans persistent aerial surveillance.  Wake up everyone you know... apathy is consent.    The tyrants win when we stay silent.  We are at the fork in the road....  One path: freedom, messy and human.  The other: the machine state where you are never alone and never truly free.    Choose. And choose fast...  The drones are already flying. Read through the responses, engage thoughtfully, ask questions, challenge ideas respectfully, and contribute to meaningful, productive discussion.  And if you're not already following  @TrueOnX... linkedin.com/posts/datafying…  Maybe the real gap here is that the verification loop never closes. Numbers get built and handed off, but nobody keeps tracking how they actually get used, or where the definitions quietly drift out of shape along the way. And the data team is the only group positioned to trace that.  There's also what happens next. AI is going to make it possible for every department to run its own analysis, and that seems close to certain at this point. What comes with it is a pile of competing numbers across teams. Which ones are correct? Which one should we actually be acting on? Answering that company-wide feels like something only a data science team can own.

Spain are champions of the world.  After 120 minutes of tension, exhaustion and unbearable emotion, Ferran Torres broke Argentina’s resistance and delivered Spain its second World Cup with a 1–0 victory in extra time.  But the World Cup has never been about one match alone.  For six weeks, the world stopped.  Different nations. Different languages. Different religions, cultures and histories. Yet for a few hours each day, billions of people felt the same anxiety, celebrated the same impossible goals and suffered through the same heartbreak.  That is the power of football.  It transcends every boundary humanity has created and reminds us that passion requires no translation.  This tournament may also have represented the final World Cup chapter for three of the greatest artists the sport has ever produced.  Lionel Messi.  Cristiano Ronaldo.  Neymar.  Different players. Different personalities. Different paths to immortality. But each gave an entire generation memories that will survive long after the final whistle. Whatever rivalries divided their supporters, greatness deserves to be recognized while it is still standing in front of us.  Today, however, belongs to Spain.  The last time Spain lifted this trophy, they possessed a team that almost felt unfair.  Iker Casillas behind Sergio Ramos, Carles Puyol, Gerard Piqué and Joan Capdevila. Sergio Busquets and Xabi Alonso controlling the center. Xavi conducting the orchestra. Andrés Iniesta finding spaces nobody else could see. David Villa finishing everything in front of him.  That generation did not simply win matches. They changed how the game was understood.  Sixteen years later, Spain returned with a different rhythm and performed a different dance.  This was not a recreation of 2010. It was the beginning of something new.  Lamine Yamal arrived as the next great force in world football. Nico Williams attacked without fear. Pedri and Gavi carried the technical identity of Spanish football into another era. Rodri provided the intelligence and authority. Ferran Torres delivered the moment that will live forever.  The torch has officially been passed.  Messi, Ronaldo and Neymar represented the age we grew up watching.  Now Lamine Yamal, Erling Haaland and the next generation are beginning to shape one of their own.  The names change. The nations rise and fall. New heroes replace the old ones.  But the feeling remains eternal.  Spain are world champions.  Football belongs to everyone.

Just in: More than 300,000 accounts in South Korea have been forced to liquidate their positions in full by securities firms. From "earning five years' salary in vain" to "losing all their savings in five years".  From July 1 to July 13, South Korea's storage giants fell sharply. SK Hynix retraced nearly 40% from its high point, and Samsung Electronics retraced nearly 30% from its high point. A wave of forced liquidations swept the Korean stock market.  According to Brokerage China citing official Korean statistics on July 19, as of July 13, the cumulative forced liquidation scale in July reached 344.2 billion won (approximately RMB 1.57 billion). On July 9 alone, the scale reached 144.2 billion won. More than 1.2 million leveraged retail accounts in the market have hit the margin call line.  About 320,000 to 360,000 accounts have been forced to liquidate in full by brokers, and some accounts even owe brokers funds. According to the Financial Associated Press, South Korea's population aged 15 to 64 is about 35.7 million. Based on this estimate, about 1 in 30 working-age people will receive a margin call.  According to reports, margin balances in retail brokerage accounts have dropped by about 30 trillion won to the lowest level since February 20 this year. At the same time, the proportion of accounts receiving margin calls rose to about 5% on Friday, and is expected to rise further as the sell-off in the semiconductor sector intensifies.  In June 2026, a Korean girl said: "This is the best summer of my adult life. I found a job, earned five years' salary in the stock market for free, have a passport to fly around the world, and can go swimming in the island with discounted air tickets and a week's salary." But on July 7, the joke suddenly changed.  A Korean boy said: "This is the worst summer in my adult life. I was jealous of my colleagues' money, so I threw all my savings into the stock market. I increased the leverage by five times. When the circuit breaker hit, my position was liquidated.  I lost five years of savings, the Korean won devalued by 20%, and I can't even pay for next week's rent." Capital flow data on Thursday showed foreign and local institutional investors were already pulling away. The South Korean stock market was closed on Friday due to a legal holiday.  According to a recent report by Xinhua News Agency, the South Korean government regards semiconductor companies as the core of South Korea's future competitiveness.  From government decision-making, corporate investment to capital markets, South Korea has gradually formed a development path in which the whole country is betting on semiconductors and emerging technology industries.  Recently, the South Korean government announced national “three major super projects” with a total investment of 1,461 trillion won (approximately 1,496 won for 1 U.S. dollar), focusing on the construction of semiconductors, physical artificial intelligence (AI), and AI data centers.  Samsung Electronics and SK Group also announced long-term investment plans totaling approximately 4,755 trillion won, further strengthening South Korea’s strategy of building a global AI industry center. This national strategy quickly triggered a "resonance" in the capital market and began to change the way South Korean people allocate their wealth.  During a recent interview in Seoul, reporters found that whether it was in securities companies, office buildings, restaurants, or university campuses, the most talked about topic was stocks. "Have you opened an account?" has almost become a new greeting among young people in South Korea.  During lunch break, many Korean office workers stare at their mobile phones to update the KOSPI trend while eating.  Data from the Korean Financial Supervisory Service and other institutions show that in the first five months of this year, the number of Korean pension savings insurance cancellations increased by 62.7% year-on-year, and the scale of fund redemptions surged by 146.1% year-on-year.  A large amount of funds originally used for retirement and long-term investment flowed to the stock market. South Korea's five largest commercial banks have consumed more than 85% of the annual household loan quota in the first half of the year.  One of the important reasons for the growth of credit loans is residents "borrowing money to speculate in stocks." A month ago, some Korean investors were still cheering for "the most dreamy summer of their lives." Just a few weeks later, some investors said that they had invested all their savings and had liquidated their positions after the recent sharp decline.  Analysts pointed out that when "if you miss it, you will fall behind" becomes the common mentality in Korean society, the rise and fall of the market will be driven more by emotions, expectations and leveraged funds.  South Korean public opinion has begun to reflect on whether this capital carnival with the participation of all people amid the boom in technology investment is turning investment into a survival game that "cannot lose". The stock market continues to fluctuate violently, prompting South Korean regulatory authorities to take substantive action.  The Korean government has recently held consecutive meetings to discuss investment leverage risks and countermeasures.  The market expects that regulators will study measures such as increasing margin requirements, adjusting leverage multiples, and limiting excessive product fluctuations to reduce the amplifying effect of leveraged transactions on market fluctuations.  However, many Korean industry insiders believe that these measures are more like delaying the release of risks rather than eliminating the risks themselves.  Lee Can-jin, president of the Korea Financial Supervisory Service, said that related issues “are not something that can be solved at once, but are areas that require continuous correction and improvement.” South Korea's Chosun Ilbo commented that this reflects that regulatory authorities are still difficult to come up with solutions that can fundamentally stabilize the market.  On the one hand, a large number of individual investors have invested huge sums of money to purchase related leveraged products. If they are directly suspended or delisted, it may trigger a market impact and cause greater losses to investors.  On the other hand, single-stock exchange-traded open-end index funds (ETFs) were launched after multiple government departments promoted and revised relevant regulations. If they are completely rejected now, it will definitely affect the credibility of government supervision.  For South Korea, AI and semiconductors are still strategic industries with high international competitiveness in the future. But when policy support, industrial capital, and residents’ wealth allocation all “bet” on a single track at the same time, market expectations will continue to amplify, bringing extreme market risks.  Analysts pointed out that the real problem that needs to be solved in the future is how the government can strengthen investor education and guidance to return investment to industrial value and economic fundamentals.  #Stocks #AI #Semiconductors #Earnings  Read more: kgwv.com/tweets/posts/2026-0…

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Critical WordPress Pre-Auth Exploit Chain: WP2Shell A newly disclosed exploit chain, WP2Shell, allows attackers to fully compromise vulnerable WordPress sites without authentication. ThreatMon IntelHub has released a free detection tool to help identify exposed instances.  🔎 Check your site: ThreatMon IntelHub - Free Security Tools  The vulnerability chain 🔸 CVE-2026-63030 – Route confusion in the REST /batch/v1 endpoint 🔸 CVE-2026-60137 – SQL Injection via WP_Query::author__not_in Potential impact An unauthenticated attacker can: ✅ Verify the SQL injection remotely ✅ Create a new administrator account ✅ Upload a malicious plugin ✅ Execute arbitrary commands on the server ✅ Achieve full site compromise Affected versions: WordPress 6.8.0 – 7.0.1 About the detection tool ✔️ Detection-only (non-intrusive) ✔️ No account required ✔️ Free to use Recommended actions 🔹 Update WordPress to the latest patched release immediately. 🔹 Review recently created administrator accounts. 🔹 Audit installed and active plugins for anything unfamiliar. 🔹 Restrict access to /wp-json/batch/v1 and ?rest_route=/batch/v1 at the edge until systems are patched. ⏱️ It takes less than 10 seconds to check whether your site is exposed.  #WordPress #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #CVE #WP2Shell #SQLInjection #PreAuth #RCE

⚠️ When two "medium-looking" vulnerabilities meet, the outcome isn't always medium.  The recently disclosed wp2shell chain is a good example of why vulnerabilities should be evaluated as attack paths rather than isolated CVEs.  A REST API authorization bypass (CVE-2026-63030) removes the authentication barrier. A separate SQL injection (CVE-2026-60137) suddenly becomes reachable. Neither issue tells the full story on its own, but together they can lead to administrative compromise and, ultimately, remote code execution.  What makes this case stand out is that the vulnerabilities affect WordPress Core, not a third-party plugin or theme.  In our latest blog, we explain:  • How the exploit chain works  • Which WordPress versions are affected  • Why the combined risk is much higher than the individual CVSS scores suggest  • What defenders should verify after applying the patches  If you're responsible for WordPress environments, this is worth reviewing.  ➡️ threatmon.io/inside-wp2shell… #WordPress #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ApplicationSecurity #RCE #CVE

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 New "WP2Shell" Vulnerability Enables Unauthenticated RCE in WordPress Core  Security researchers have disclosed WP2Shell, a critical vulnerability affecting WordPress core that can allow unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on vulnerable websites.  Unlike most WordPress attacks, this flaw does not rely on a vulnerable plugin or theme—it exists in WordPress core itself.  Affected versions:  * WordPress 6.9.0 – 6.9.4 * WordPress 7.0.0 – 7.0.1  Patched versions:  * WordPress 6.9.5 * WordPress 7.0.2 and later  Organizations running affected versions should:  * Update WordPress immediately. * Verify automatic updates completed successfully. * Review web server logs for suspicious requests targeting `/wp-json/batch/v1`. * Investigate any internet-facing, unpatched instances for signs of compromise.  💡 Analyst Note: Core vulnerabilities are significantly more impactful than plugin vulnerabilities because they can affect millions of WordPress installations simultaneously. Organizations should prioritize patching immediately, as proof-of-concept exploits often appear shortly after public disclosure.  #DDW #CyberSecurity #WordPress #Vulnerability wp2shell.com/

🚨 Alleged Just Eat Database Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing approximately 90 million Just Eat user records on a cybercrime forum.  According to the listing, the alleged dataset includes: * User IDs * Email addresses * Password hashes * Full names * Country information * Currency codes * Account types * Membership dates * Email verification status * Campaign activity * Additional account metadata  The post includes a sample of the purported database but does not provide sufficient evidence to independently verify the authenticity, origin, or recency of the data. Daily Dark Web has not confirmed that the data originated from a compromise of Just Eat or Just Eat Takeaway.com.  Analyst Note: Large consumer database claims are common on cybercrime forums. Many are recycled, aggregated from previous breaches, or artificially inflated. Organizations and users should wait for technical validation before assuming a new compromise has occurred.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #JustEat

🇫🇷 Alleged Stickers-Moto.fr Database Posted on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor has published what they describe as a small SQL database belonging to Stickers-Moto.fr, a French online retailer specializing in motorcycle stickers and accessories.  According to the forum post, the leaked files allegedly include: * User records (Users.csv) * Order history (Commandes.csv) * Session-related data * Customer account information  The post suggests the dataset is relatively small and includes sample records from multiple CSV files. At the time of writing, Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the leaked data or confirmed that it originated from a compromise of Stickers-Moto.fr.  Analyst Note: Even small e-commerce database leaks can expose valuable customer information that enables phishing, credential stuffing, and account takeover attacks, particularly if users have reused passwords across multiple services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇲🇽 Alleged CECyTE Tabasco Database Leaked  A threat actor has posted what they claim is the full database of CECyTE Tabasco (Colegio de Estudios Científicos y Tecnológicos del Estado de Tabasco), a public educational institution in Mexico.  According to the post, the dataset allegedly includes: * RFC (Mexican Tax ID) * CURP (National ID) * Gmail/email addresses * Usernames * Password hashes * Full names and related account information  The listing references the institution's website (cecytab.edu.mx) and advertises the database as a complete dump. At the time of writing, Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the data or confirmed whether it originated from a compromise of CECyTE Tabasco.  Analyst Note: Educational institutions continue to be attractive targets due to the large volume of student, faculty, and administrative records they maintain. Even when passwords are stored as hashes, exposed identity data such as CURP and RFC can significantly increase the risk of identity theft and phishing.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇮🇩 Alleged Indonesian Government Cyber Division Access Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is access to an Indonesian government cyber division/police environment on a cybercrime forum.  According to the listing: * Claimed target: Indonesian Government Cyber Division / Police * Access type: Unspecified network or system access * Asking price: $300 * Escrow services accepted * Buyers instructed to contact the seller privately  The listing provides virtually no technical details, proof of access, screenshots, or evidence supporting the claim. As a result, the authenticity, scope, and impact of the alleged access cannot be independently verified.  Analyst Note: Government access advertisements frequently appear on cybercrime forums, but many are fraudulent, outdated, or significantly exaggerated. Organizations should treat such listings as potential early warning indicators while awaiting technical validation or official confirmation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Indonesia

🇻🇳 Alleged Database from Vietnamese Website Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a fresh database from **wasaki.vn**, a Vietnam-based website.  According to the sample shared in the listing, the dataset allegedly includes: * User IDs * Usernames * Password hashes * Email addresses * Display names * Registration timestamps * Account activation keys * User roles * WordPress account metadata  The exposed schema appears consistent with a WordPress user table, suggesting the listing may contain user account information and administrative accounts. However, the authenticity, origin, and recency of the dataset have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from the organization regarding a security incident matching these claims.  Analyst Note: WordPress-based websites remain frequent targets for attackers exploiting vulnerable plugins, themes, weak credentials, or outdated installations. Even when passwords are hashed, exposed account databases can facilitate credential stuffing, phishing, and further attacks if password reuse is involved.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Vietnam

According to the sample shared in the listing, the dataset allegedly contains: * User IDs * Usernames * Password hashes * Email addresses * First and last names * Identification numbers * Phone numbers * Institutional and department information * Physical addresses * City and country * Language and timezone settings * Account status and policy information  The post includes a sample of the alleged records and download links. However, the authenticity, origin, and recency of the dataset have not been independently verified. If confirmed, a compromise of this nature could expose users to credential stuffing, phishing, and identity theft, particularly if passwords were reused across multiple services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

🇲🇽 400,327 Mexican Government Supplier Records Allegedly Exposed  A threat actor is claiming that an exposed database belonging to **DIF Tamaulipas** (Sistema para el Desarrollo Integral de la Familia), a family services agency under the Government of Tamaulipas, Mexico, contains information on more than **400,000 supplier registrations**.  According to the post, the exposed records allegedly include: * Supplier names (individuals and companies) * Mexican Tax IDs (RFC) * Full physical addresses * Municipalities and states * Legal representative names * Business categories * Contact phone numbers * Email addresses * Internal registration numbers  The actor claims the database was accessible without authentication and provides a description of the exposed fields. However, the authenticity of the data, the exposure method, and whether the database remains publicly accessible have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there has been no public confirmation from DIF Tamaulipas regarding the alleged exposure.  Analyst Note: Government supplier databases often contain sensitive business and personally identifiable information. If confirmed, exposures of this nature can increase the risk of procurement fraud, business email compromise (BEC), identity theft, and targeted phishing campaigns against registered vendors.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇮🇩 Alleged Indonesian National Vaccine Database Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a second database from Indonesia's national vaccine system.  According to the listing, the dataset allegedly contains: * National Identification Numbers (NIK) * Full names * Places and dates of birth * Residential addresses * Gender * Telephone numbers * District and subdistrict information * Administrative records * Creation and update timestamps  The forum post describes the data as a "second database" and includes a sample of the alleged records. However, the authenticity, origin, and scope of the dataset have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from Indonesian authorities regarding a breach matching these claims.  Analyst Note: Government health databases remain attractive targets due to the volume of personally identifiable information they contain. However, threat actors frequently repackage or relist previously leaked datasets, making independent validation essential before drawing conclusions.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Indonesia

🇬🇹 Alleged Guatemalan Citizen Database Advertised with Election-Related Claims  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing personal information on Guatemalan citizens, while also alleging, without providing evidence, that the data was used to manipulate Guatemala's 2023 elections.  According to the listing, the dataset allegedly contains: * National identification numbers * Full names * Dates of birth * Tax identification numbers * Email addresses * Profile photographs (for some individuals)  The post includes a sample of the alleged data and download links. However, the authenticity of the dataset and the accompanying claims regarding election manipulation have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no publicly available evidence confirming that this dataset was involved in Guatemala's 2023 election process or that it originated from an election-related system.  Analyst Note: Threat actors frequently attach sensational political narratives to leaked datasets to increase visibility and perceived value. The existence of a dataset does not substantiate broader claims of election fraud or interference, which require independent forensic and investigative evidence.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Guatemala

🇧🇷 Alleged 20,000-Record Database from Brazilian E-Commerce Site Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a fresh database from **lojanegociosdigital.com.br**, a Brazilian e-commerce platform, containing approximately **20,000 customer records**.  According to the listing and sample, the dataset allegedly includes: * Customer IDs * Billing email addresses * Order and transaction IDs * Payment method details * Order status and timestamps * Billing and IP addresses * Currency and tax information * WooCommerce order metadata  The forum post labels the dataset as a recent leak and requires forum credits to access the download. However, the authenticity, origin, and freshness of the data have not been independently verified.  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from the organization regarding a security incident matching these claims.  Analyst Note: Smaller e-commerce platforms running WooCommerce remain frequent targets for attackers exploiting vulnerable plugins, weak administrative credentials, or outdated software. Until independently validated, the claimed compromise should be treated as unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-20 07:56 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 AI를 활용한 공격 자동화와 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)이 결합된 형태로 진화하고 있습니다. 특히, 악성 npm 패키지 유포나 소프트웨어 업데이트 메커니즘을 악용한 침투가 빈번하며, 기업의 개발자 채용 프로세스를 우회하여 악의적인 행위자를 내부자로 침투시키는 사례가 보고되었습니다([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/hackers-abuse-vipnet-software-to-target-russian-govt-agencies/)). 또한, SonicWall SMA와 같은 VPN 장비의 Zero-Day 취약점을 악용한 랜섬웨어 공격이 급증하고 있어, 경계 보안 장비의 패치 관리가 시급한 상황입니다([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/inc-ransomware-exploits-sonicwall-sma-zero-days)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **UAC-0145**: ClickFix CAPTCHA 기법을 활용하여 우크라이나 대상 데이터 탈취형 악성코드 유포([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/uac-0145-uses-clickfix-captchas-to.html))
- **Lazarus Group**: 암호화폐 탈취 및 개발자 채용 위장 침투, npm 악성 패키지(chai-as-init) 유포 등 지속적인 공급망 공격([Securelist](https://securelist.com/tr/hellonet-vipnet/120700/), [이스트시큐리티](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766))
- **Cavern Manticore**: 이란 연계 APT 그룹으로, 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 사용하여 이스라엘 등 타겟 공격([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/))
- **Inc Ransomware**: SonicWall SMA Zero-Day 취약점을 악용하여 기업 네트워크에 침투 및 랜섬웨어 배포([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/inc-ransomware-exploits-sonicwall-sma-zero-days))
- **GoSerpent**: 동남아시아 정부 기관을 타겟으로 하는 데이터 수집 및 유출 목적의 백도어 캠페인([Securelist](https://securelist.com/goserpent-backdoor-in-southeast-asia/120687/))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **공급망 위협**: 북한발 악성 npm 패키지 'chai-as-init'이 유포되어 국내 개발 환경 및 공급망 보안에 주의가 요구됨([이스트시큐리티](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766))
- **피싱 공격**: 국세청 사칭 피싱 메일을 통해 원격제어 악성코드를 유포하는 사례 확인([이스트시큐리티](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5771))
- **기타**: 한국 특정 위협은 아니나, 국내 보안 기업(AhnLab)이 리눅스 서버 대상 코인 마이너 유포 및 인포스틸러 동향을 지속 모니터링 중([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94483/))

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-42533**: NGINX Worker Process Heap Buffer Overflow, 원격 코드 실행 가능([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/critical-nginx-vulnerability-can-crash.html))
- **SonicWall SMA Zero-Days**: VPN 장비 대상 Root 권한 획득 취약점([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/inc-ransomware-exploits-sonicwall-sma-zero-days))
- **WordPress Core "wp2shell"**: RCE 취약점, 공개 익스플로잇 존재([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/wordpress-core-wp2shell-rce-flaws-get-public-exploits-patch-now/))
- **7-Zip RCE**: 악성 압축 파일 실행 시 코드 실행 취약점([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/update-now-7-zip-fixes-rce-flaw-exploitable-with-malicious-archives/))

### 5. IOC 요약
- **도메인**: 없음
- **해시**: 없음
- **CVE**: 
    - CVE-2026-42533 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/critical-nginx-vulnerability-can-crash.html))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **VPN 및 Edge 장비 점검**: SonicWall SMA 등 VPN 장비의 최신 보안 패치 적용 여부를 즉시 확인하고, 노출된 관리자 페이지를 차단하십시오.
- **개발 환경 보안 강화**: npm 등 오픈소스 패키지 사용 시 출처를 엄격히 검증하고, 'chai-as-init'과 같은 의심스러운 패키지 사용 여부를 전수 조사하십시오.
- **피싱 대응**: 국세청 등 공공기관을 사칭한 이메일 내 링크 클릭 및 실행 파일 다운로드를 금지하고, 임직원 대상 보안 교육을 실시하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8/10
- **근거**: VPN Zero-Day 악용 및 공급망 공격이 활발하며, 특히 기업 내부 개발 환경을 직접 타겟팅하는 공격이 지속되고 있어 매우 위험함.

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 14,351개 (입력 12,969 / 출력 1,382)
📈 세션 누적: 14,351 / 1,000,000 (1.4%) | 세션 잔여 추정 985,649개
